questions.text: In which year did Mahatma Gandhi return to India from South Africa?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi returned to India in January 1915 after successfully leading a non-violent protest in South Africa.

questions.text: The Rowlatt Act of 1919 allowed the government to:
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Act gave the British government enormous powers to repress political activities and allowed detention without trial for up to two years.

questions.text: Who was the author of the book 'Hind Swaraj'?
questions.explanation: In 'Hind Swaraj' (1909), Mahatma Gandhi declared that British rule was established in India with the cooperation of Indians.

questions.text: What led to the withdrawal of the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1922?
questions.explanation: Gandhiji called off the movement after a violent incident at Chauri Chaura where a police station was set on fire by protesters.

questions.text: Which movement was started by the 'Dandi March'?
questions.explanation: The Civil Disobedience Movement began in 1930 when Gandhiji reached Dandi and ceremonially violated the salt law.
